name: Kaeng Paa Kai (Jungle Curry with Chicken)
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons takrai (lemon grass), bruised and thinly sliced
1/4 cup kachai (lesser ginger), peeled and chopped
8 prik chee fa daegn haeng (dried red Thai jalapenos), crushed
1/4 cup prik che fa (Thai jalapenos), julienned
1/2 cup bai maenglak (kaffir lime leaves), shredded
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the curry paste by grinding all ingredients to a fine paste in a mortar and pestle or food processor.
2. Pierce the coconuts and drain the juice into a pitcher. Chop off the top of each coconut, just above the midpoint, leaving four serving bowls.
3. Scoop out the coconut "meat" in leaf-shaped pieces with a spoon (or melon baller). Add about half a cup of coconut to the juice for every two cups of juice and refrigerate.
4. Reserve half a cup of coconut meat for garnish and the rest to make coconut milk for other recipes.
5. Heat a wok or large sautee pan over medium-high heat, add a little oil, and stir fry the curry paste until aromatic.
6. Add the chicken and stir fry briefly, then add the remaining ingredients (except lime leaves and chicken stock) and stir fry until the chicken changes color.
7. Add the stock and cover; simmer until the chicken and eggplant are cooked through.
8. Serve the curry in the four large coconut shell bowls, garnished with lime leaves, accompanied by rice, and bring chilled coconut nectar as a refreshing drink.
9. Accompany with usual condiments (nam pla prik, dried ground chilis, and sugar).
